message:
fix(identity): prioritize id_token over access_token in Codex extraction

Refactor findCodexToken to check for id_token and idToken in the nested
tokens object earlier in the priority order, before falling back to
access_token variants. This ensures that when both are present, we
extract the more useful id_token which contains identity claims (email,
name, etc.) for user identification.

The id_token is a JWT that contains identity claims from the OpenID
Connect flow, while access_token is an opaque bearer token used for API
authorization. For identity extraction purposes, the id_token is strictly
preferred as it contains parseable user information.

Previous order:
1. id_token (top-level) / idToken (top-level)
2. access_token (top-level) / accessToken (top-level) / token (top-level)
3. tokens.id_token / tokens.idToken / tokens.access_token / tokens.accessToken

New order:
1. id_token (top-level) / idToken (top-level)
2. tokens.id_token / tokens.idToken  <-- MOVED UP
3. access_token (top-level) / accessToken (top-level) / token (top-level)
4. tokens.access_token / tokens.accessToken

This also consolidates the tokens map lookup to avoid redundant type
assertions, improving code clarity and reducing potential nil pointer
issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
